examples.da: Selvom det regner, går vi en tur.
examples.en: Even though it's raining, we're going for a walk.

examples.da: Han kom, selvom han var træt.
examples.en: He came even though he was tired.

selvom.gotcha: Officially written as two words: "selv om". The one-word form is very common but informal.

examples.da: Jeg kommer ikke, jeg er nemlig syg.
examples.en: I'm not coming, because I'm sick, you see.

examples.da: Nemlig! Det var lige det, jeg mente.
examples.en: Exactly! That's just what I meant.

nemlig.gotcha: Often just adds "you see / as you know" to a sentence, or means "exactly!" as a reply.

examples.da: Hvilket tidspunkt passer dig bedst?
examples.en: What time suits you best?

examples.da: På det tidspunkt boede vi i Aarhus.
examples.en: At that time we lived in Aarhus.

examples.da: Vi flytter til København næste måned.
examples.en: We are moving to Copenhagen next month.

examples.da: Kan du flytte din bil?
examples.en: Can you move your car?

flytte.gotcha: Use "flytte" for relocating things or homes; use "bevæge" for moving your body.

examples.da: Jeg har købt en brugt bil.
examples.en: I have bought a used car.

examples.da: Cyklen er brugt, men den virker fint.
examples.en: The bike is second-hand, but it works fine.

brugt.gotcha: Also the past participle of "bruge" (to use): "Jeg har brugt pengene".

examples.da: Vores fly letter klokken syv.
examples.en: Our plane takes off at seven o'clock.

examples.da: Jeg tager et fly til London.
examples.en: I'm taking a plane to London.

fly.gotcha: Same form in singular and plural: et fly, to fly.

examples.da: Der er ingen fare på færde.
examples.en: There is no danger.

examples.da: Hun fór ud af døren.
examples.en: She rushed out of the door.

fare.gotcha: Both a noun (danger) and an irregular verb (fare, fór/for, faret) meaning to rush.

examples.da: Det er en rigtig god ide.
examples.en: That's a really good idea.

examples.da: Har du en bedre ide?
examples.en: Do you have a better idea?

ide.gotcha: Usually written "idé" with accent; stress is on the last syllable.

examples.da: De serverer vildt på restauranten om efteråret.
examples.en: They serve game at the restaurant in autumn.

examples.da: Vi spiste vildt med kartofler.
examples.en: We ate venison with potatoes.

vildt.gotcha: Looks like the neuter of "vild" (wild); in speech "vildt" is also slang for "really".

examples.da: Vil du have en øl?
examples.en: Do you want a beer?

examples.da: Øllet er koldt.
examples.en: The beer is cold.

øl.gotcha: The liquid is "et øl / øllet", but one serving is "en øl". Plural is also "øl".

examples.da: Slip min hånd!
examples.en: Let go of my hand!

examples.da: Jeg slap for at vaske op.
examples.en: I got out of doing the dishes.

slippe.gotcha: Irregular: slippe, slap, sluppet. "Slippe for" means to avoid or get out of something.

examples.da: Fuck, jeg glemte mine nøgler!
examples.en: Damn, I forgot my keys!

fuck.gotcha: Borrowed from English but feels milder in Danish; still informal.

examples.da: Kender du min søster?
examples.en: Do you know my sister?

examples.da: Jeg kender ikke byen så godt.
examples.en: I don't know the city very well.

kende.gotcha: "Kende" is knowing people/places; "vide" is knowing facts. Irregular: kende, kendte, kendt.

examples.da: Jeg står tidligt op hver dag.
examples.en: I get up early every day.

examples.da: Vi kom for tidligt til festen.
examples.en: We arrived too early for the party.

tidligt.gotcha: The d is silent: pronounced roughly "tee-lit".

examples.da: En ældre dame spurgte om vej.
examples.en: An elderly lady asked for directions.

examples.da: Der står en dame ved døren.
examples.en: There is a lady at the door.

examples.da: Er du helt skør?
examples.en: Are you completely crazy?

examples.da: Det var en skør ide.
examples.en: That was a crazy idea.

examples.da: Jeg er stolt af dig.
examples.en: I am proud of you.

examples.da: Hun var stolt over sit arbejde.
examples.en: She was proud of her work.

examples.da: Hun er en hurtig løber.
examples.en: She is a fast runner.

examples.da: Løberen vandt maratonløbet.
examples.en: The runner won the marathon.

løber.gotcha: Also the present tense of "løbe" (to run): "han løber hver dag".

examples.da: Vi laver frikadeller af fars.
examples.en: We make meatballs from minced meat.

examples.da: Køb et halvt kilo fars til i aften.
examples.en: Buy half a kilo of minced meat for tonight.

fars.gotcha: Also the genitive of "far": "fars bil" means dad's car.

examples.da: Han virkede lidt sat efter frokosten.
examples.en: He seemed a bit sluggish after lunch.

sat.gotcha: Usually met as the past participle of "sætte" (to put): "jeg har sat den her".

examples.da: Læg bogen på bordet.
examples.en: Put the book on the table.

examples.da: Jeg lagde nøglerne i skuffen.
examples.en: I put the keys in the drawer.

lægge.gotcha: Irregular: lægge, lagde, lagt. Easily confused with "ligge" (to lie, be lying).

examples.da: Det er rent umuligt at finde parkering her.
examples.en: It is quite impossible to find parking here.

examples.da: Jeg glemte det rent og skært.
examples.en: I plain forgot it.

rent.gotcha: False friend: not English "rent" (money for housing) — that is "husleje".

examples.da: Jeg føler mig træt i dag.
examples.en: I feel tired today.

examples.da: Hvordan føler du dig?
examples.en: How do you feel?

føle.gotcha: About your own state it is reflexive: "jeg føler mig glad", not "jeg føler glad".

examples.da: Enhver kan lære at svømme.
examples.en: Anyone can learn to swim.

examples.da: Det ved enhver dansker.
examples.en: Every Dane knows that.

examples.da: Det var netop det, jeg sagde.
examples.en: That is exactly what I said.

examples.da: Toget er netop kørt.
examples.en: The train has just left.

examples.da: Luk munden, når du spiser.
examples.en: Close your mouth when you eat.

examples.da: Hun havde mad i munden.
examples.en: She had food in her mouth.

mund.gotcha: The d is silent: pronounced roughly "mon".

examples.da: Hej makker, hvordan går det?
examples.en: Hey buddy, how are you?

examples.da: Han er min makker i kortspillet.
examples.en: He is my partner in the card game.

examples.da: Skal vi tage en drink i baren?
examples.en: Shall we have a drink at the bar?

examples.da: Hun bestilte en drink med rom.
examples.en: She ordered a cocktail with rum.

drink.gotcha: False friend: in Danish only a mixed alcoholic drink. A soft drink or beer is not "en drink".

examples.da: Hvor pokker er mine nøgler?
examples.en: Where the heck are my keys?

examples.da: Pokkers, jeg glemte mødet!
examples.en: Darn, I forgot the meeting!

pokker.gotcha: Mild curse word, roughly like "heck" or "darn".

examples.da: Hvad er prisen på den her trøje?
examples.en: What is the price of this sweater?

examples.da: Hun vandt en pris for sin bog.
examples.en: She won a prize for her book.

examples.da: Alting var stille i huset.
examples.en: Everything was quiet in the house.

examples.da: Hun bekymrer sig om alting.
examples.en: She worries about everything.

examples.da: Vi vil sælge vores bil.
examples.en: We want to sell our car.

examples.da: Han solgte huset sidste år.
examples.en: He sold the house last year.

examples.da: Katten fangede en mus.
examples.en: The cat caught a mouse.

examples.da: Fangen flygtede fra fængslet.
examples.en: The prisoner escaped from the prison.

fange.gotcha: Same word is a verb (to catch) and a noun (prisoner).

examples.da: Pas på, du ikke ødelægger telefonen.
examples.en: Be careful you don't break the phone.

examples.da: Regnen ødelagde vores picnic.
examples.en: The rain ruined our picnic.

examples.da: Hun har en smuk stemme.
examples.en: She has a beautiful voice.

examples.da: Jeg stemmer på søndag.
examples.en: I am voting on Sunday.

stemme.gotcha: "Det stemmer" is a fixed phrase meaning "that's correct".

examples.da: Kig på det her billede.
examples.en: Look at this picture.

examples.da: Hun kiggede ud ad vinduet.
examples.en: She looked out of the window.

kigge.gotcha: Pronounced "kikke" — the g sounds like k.

examples.da: Håndklædet er helt tørt.
examples.en: The towel is completely dry.

examples.da: Jorden er meget tør i sommer.
examples.en: The soil is very dry this summer.

tør.gotcha: Also the present tense of "turde" (to dare): "jeg tør ikke" = I don't dare.

examples.da: Det er løgn, det hele!
examples.en: It's all a lie!

examples.da: Han fortalte en løgn om sin alder.
examples.en: He told a lie about his age.

løgn.gotcha: The g is pronounced like a j-sound: roughly "loyn".

examples.da: Jeg har købt en gave til dig.
examples.en: I have bought a present for you.

examples.da: Tak for den fine gave!
examples.en: Thank you for the lovely gift!

gave.gotcha: False friend: looks like English "gave" (past of give), but it is a noun.

examples.da: Motion er godt for kroppen.
examples.en: Exercise is good for the body.

examples.da: Han fryser over hele kroppen.
examples.en: He is cold all over his body.

examples.da: Vi har mistet kontakten til hinanden.
examples.en: We have lost contact with each other.

examples.da: Sæt opladeren i kontakten.
examples.en: Plug the charger into the socket.

kontakt.gotcha: Also means a power outlet: "stikkontakt" is a wall socket.

examples.da: Hvorfor er du så sur i dag?
examples.en: Why are you so grumpy today?

examples.da: Citronen smager surt.
examples.en: The lemon tastes sour.

sur.gotcha: About people it means grumpy or annoyed, not sour.

examples.da: Det er en stor ære at være her.
examples.en: It is a great honor to be here.

examples.da: Han kæmpede for sin ære.
examples.en: He fought for his honor.

examples.da: Vær forsigtig på den glatte vej.
examples.en: Be careful on the slippery road.

examples.da: Hun åbnede æsken meget forsigtigt.
examples.en: She opened the box very carefully.

examples.da: Filmen starter klokken otte.
examples.en: The movie starts at eight o'clock.

examples.da: Bilen vil ikke starte.
examples.en: The car won't start.

examples.da: Jeg skal lige ordne noget i køkkenet.
examples.en: I just have to sort something out in the kitchen.

examples.da: Kan du ordne det for mig?
examples.en: Can you take care of that for me?

examples.da: Hvem er præsident i USA nu?
examples.en: Who is president of the USA now?

examples.da: Præsidenten holdt en lang tale.
examples.en: The president gave a long speech.

examples.da: Glem ikke din jakke.
examples.en: Don't forget your jacket.

examples.da: Jeg har glemt hans navn.
examples.en: I have forgotten his name.

examples.da: Han er stærk nok til at løfte kassen.
examples.en: He is strong enough to lift the box.

examples.da: Maden er for stærk for mig.
examples.en: The food is too spicy for me.

stærk.gotcha: About food it means spicy, not strong.

examples.da: Vandet løber gennem et rør.
examples.en: The water runs through a pipe.

examples.da: Røret under vasken er utæt.
examples.en: The pipe under the sink is leaking.

rør.gotcha: Also the imperative of "røre": "rør den ikke!" means "don't touch it!"

examples.da: Pas på, du ikke mister dine nøgler.
examples.en: Be careful you don't lose your keys.

examples.da: Han mistede sit job sidste år.
examples.en: He lost his job last year.

miste.gotcha: "Miste" is losing something for good; "tabe" is dropping or losing a game.

examples.da: Vi sang en sang for hende.
examples.en: We sang a song for her.

examples.da: Det er min yndlingssang.
examples.en: That is my favorite song.

sang.gotcha: Also the past tense of "synge" (to sing).

examples.da: Hun har sin helt egen stil.
examples.en: She has her very own style.

examples.da: Vi skal skrive en stil i skolen.
examples.en: We have to write an essay at school.

stil.gotcha: In school contexts it means an essay, not style.

examples.da: Der var pænt mange mennesker til festen.
examples.en: There were quite a lot of people at the party.

examples.da: Huset kostede pænt over en million.
examples.en: The house cost well over a million.

examples.da: Vi mødes klokken halv tre.
examples.en: We meet at half past two.

examples.da: Jeg spiste en halv pizza.
examples.en: I ate half a pizza.

halv.gotcha: "Halv tre" means 2:30, not 3:30 — half an hour BEFORE the hour.

examples.da: Butikken er åben til klokken otte.
examples.en: The shop is open until eight o'clock.

examples.da: Døren stod åben hele natten.
examples.en: The door stood open all night.

examples.da: Naturligvis hjælper jeg dig.
examples.en: Of course I will help you.

examples.da: Du må naturligvis gerne komme med.
examples.en: You are of course welcome to come along.

examples.da: Byen blev udsat for et angreb.
examples.en: The city was subjected to an attack.

examples.da: Holdet gik til angreb i anden halvleg.
examples.en: The team went on the attack in the second half.

angreb.gotcha: Also the past tense of "angribe" (to attack).

examples.da: Jeg er en smule træt i dag.
examples.en: I am a little bit tired today.

examples.da: Kan jeg få en smule mere kaffe?
examples.en: Can I have a little more coffee?

examples.da: Det er en fin ide.
examples.en: That is a fine idea.

examples.da: Hun havde en fin kjole på.
examples.en: She was wearing a nice dress.

fin.gotcha: "Helt fint" can sound lukewarm, like English "it's fine".

examples.da: Jeg har det lidt skidt i dag.
examples.en: I'm feeling a bit poorly today.

examples.da: Skidt med det, vi finder en løsning.
examples.en: Never mind, we'll find a solution.

skidt.gotcha: The d is silent: pronounced "skit". "Skidt med det" means "never mind".

examples.da: Tony bor i Odense.
examples.en: Tony lives in Odense.

examples.da: Butikken er lukket om søndagen.
examples.en: The shop is closed on Sundays.

examples.da: Det var et lukket møde.
examples.en: It was a private meeting.

examples.da: Pludselig begyndte det at regne.
examples.en: Suddenly it started to rain.

examples.da: Han rejste sig pludseligt og gik.
examples.en: He suddenly got up and left.

examples.da: Vi kan se havet fra vores vindue.
examples.en: We can see the sea from our window.

examples.da: Danmark er omgivet af hav.
examples.en: Denmark is surrounded by sea.

hav.gotcha: Also the imperative of "have": "hav en god dag" = have a nice day.

examples.da: Jeg havde en mærkelig drøm i nat.
examples.en: I had a strange dream last night.

examples.da: Det er min store drøm at rejse jorden rundt.
examples.en: It is my big dream to travel around the world.

examples.da: Han er ryger, men vil gerne stoppe.
examples.en: He is a smoker but wants to quit.

examples.da: Der er et særligt område for rygere.
examples.en: There is a special area for smokers.

ryger.gotcha: Also present tense of "ryge"; colloquially "det ryger" can mean "it gets lost/breaks".

examples.da: Skål og tillykke med fødselsdagen!
examples.en: Cheers, and happy birthday!

examples.da: Hæld salaten op i en stor skål.
examples.en: Pour the salad into a large bowl.

skål.gotcha: Both the toast "cheers!" and the noun "bowl".

examples.da: Mary er dronning af Danmark.
examples.en: Mary is Queen of Denmark.

examples.da: Huset ligger midt i byen.
examples.en: The house is in the middle of the city.

examples.da: Han vågnede midt om natten.
examples.en: He woke up in the middle of the night.

examples.da: Jeg er så sulten, at jeg kunne spise en hest.
examples.en: I'm so hungry I could eat a horse.

examples.da: Er du sulten? Der er mad i køleskabet.
examples.en: Are you hungry? There's food in the fridge.

sulten.gotcha: An adjective despite ending in -en; it also looks like "sulten" = the hunger.

examples.da: Vi mødes på et bestemt tidspunkt.
examples.en: We meet at a specific time.

examples.da: Det vil jeg bestemt anbefale.
examples.en: I would definitely recommend that.

examples.da: Der er et direkte fly til Paris.
examples.en: There is a direct flight to Paris.

examples.da: Programmet sendes direkte i aften.
examples.en: The programme is broadcast live tonight.

direkte.gotcha: About TV and radio it means "live".

examples.da: Øh, hvad var det nu, du hed?
examples.en: Uh, what was your name again?

examples.da: Starteren i bilen er i stykker.
examples.en: The starter in the car is broken.

starter.gotcha: Also the present tense of "starte": "filmen starter nu".

examples.da: Jeg kan lide både kaffe og te.
examples.en: I like both coffee and tea.

examples.da: Hun taler både dansk og engelsk.
examples.en: She speaks both Danish and English.

både.gotcha: Also the plural of "båd" (boat): "to både" = two boats.

examples.da: Det var en interessant bog.
examples.en: That was an interesting book.

examples.da: Hun fortalte noget meget interessant.
examples.en: She said something very interesting.

examples.da: Vi bor i en lille lejlighed i byen.
examples.en: We live in a small apartment in the city.

examples.da: Jeg fik aldrig lejlighed til at takke dig.
examples.en: I never got the opportunity to thank you.

examples.da: Han opførte sig som et røvhul.
examples.en: He behaved like an asshole.

røvhul.gotcha: Very vulgar — stronger than it may feel to learners.

examples.da: Vend bøffen efter to minutter.
examples.en: Turn the steak after two minutes.

examples.da: Hun vendte sig om og smilede.
examples.en: She turned around and smiled.

vende.gotcha: Don't confuse with "vente" (to wait) — one letter apart.

examples.da: Henry arbejder på et kontor i København.
examples.en: Henry works in an office in Copenhagen.

examples.da: Jeg vil gerne undskylde for i går.
examples.en: I would like to apologize for yesterday.

examples.da: Undskyld, hvor er toilettet?
examples.en: Excuse me, where is the toilet?

examples.da: Der var mennesker overalt i byen.
examples.en: There were people everywhere in the city.

examples.da: Jeg har ledt overalt efter mine briller.
examples.en: I have looked everywhere for my glasses.

examples.da: Paul kommer på besøg i weekenden.
examples.en: Paul is coming to visit at the weekend.

examples.da: Obersten gav soldaterne en ordre.
examples.en: The colonel gave the soldiers an order.

examples.da: Min taske ligger derovre på stolen.
examples.en: My bag is over there on the chair.

examples.da: Kan du se huset derovre?
examples.en: Can you see the house over there?

examples.da: Har du styr på planerne for i morgen?
examples.en: Do you have the plans for tomorrow under control?

examples.da: Hold fast i styret på cyklen.
examples.en: Hold on to the handlebars of the bike.

styr.gotcha: Very common in the idiom "have styr på" = to have something under control.

examples.da: Nick spiller fodbold hver lørdag.
examples.en: Nick plays football every Saturday.

examples.da: Kan jeg hjælpe Dem, frue?
examples.en: Can I help you, madam?

examples.da: Hr. og fru Hansen bor på anden sal.
examples.en: Mr. and Mrs. Hansen live on the second floor.

frue.gotcha: Sounds old-fashioned or very formal in modern Danish.

examples.da: Vi ser tv hver aften.
examples.en: We watch TV every evening.

examples.da: Der er fodbold i tv i aften.
examples.en: There is football on TV tonight.

examples.da: Du var heldig med vejret i dag.
examples.en: You were lucky with the weather today.

examples.da: Hvor er du heldig!
examples.en: How lucky you are!

examples.da: Max har fødselsdag i næste uge.
examples.en: Max has a birthday next week.

examples.da: Føreren af bilen blev stoppet af politiet.
examples.en: The driver of the car was stopped by the police.

examples.da: Holdet er i førertrøjen nu.
examples.en: The team is in the lead now.

fører.gotcha: Also present tense of "føre" (to lead). "Leader" sense can echo German "Führer".

examples.da: Vi har fundet en køber til huset.
examples.en: We have found a buyer for the house.

køber.gotcha: Also the present tense of "købe": "jeg køber ind" = I do the shopping.

examples.da: Hvad laver du egentlig i weekenden?
examples.en: What are you actually doing this weekend?

examples.da: Det var egentlig ikke min ide.
examples.en: It wasn't really my idea.

egentlig.gotcha: Often just softens a question, like "by the way" or "actually".

examples.da: Sarah læser medicin i Aarhus.
examples.en: Sarah studies medicine in Aarhus.

examples.da: Han scorede to mål i kampen.
examples.en: He scored two goals in the match.

examples.da: Mit mål er at lære dansk på et år.
examples.en: My goal is to learn Danish in one year.

mål.gotcha: Also means "measurement": "tage mål" = to take measurements.

examples.da: Ordet står i RO.
examples.en: The word is listed in the official Danish spelling dictionary.

RO.gotcha: Abbreviation of Retskrivningsordbogen, the official spelling dictionary.

examples.da: Jeg har brug for lidt fred og ro.
examples.en: I need a little peace and quiet.

examples.da: Vi roede over søen i en lille båd.
examples.en: We rowed across the lake in a small boat.

ro.gotcha: Both a noun (calm) and a verb (to row).

examples.da: Himlen er helt blå i dag.
examples.en: The sky is completely blue today.

examples.da: Hun købte en blå kjole.
examples.en: She bought a blue dress.

blå.gotcha: Same form for en- and et-words: en blå bil, et blåt hus (only -t in neuter).

examples.da: Jimmy arbejder som mekaniker.
examples.en: Jimmy works as a mechanic.

examples.da: Sælgeren gav os en god pris.
examples.en: The salesman gave us a good price.

examples.da: Hun arbejder som sælger i en butik.
examples.en: She works as a salesperson in a shop.

sælger.gotcha: Also the present tense of "sælge": "han sælger biler".

examples.da: A er det første bogstav i alfabetet.
examples.en: A is the first letter of the alphabet.

examples.da: Billetterne koster 100 kroner a person.
examples.en: The tickets cost 100 kroner per person.

examples.da: Jeg hører musik, når jeg løber.
examples.en: I listen to music when I run.

examples.da: Hvilken slags musik kan du lide?
examples.en: What kind of music do you like?

musik.gotcha: Stress on the last syllable: mu-SIK.

examples.da: Han kom, skønt han var syg.
examples.en: He came, although he was ill.

examples.da: Skønt det var koldt, gik vi en tur.
examples.en: Although it was cold, we went for a walk.

skønt.gotcha: Also the neuter of "skøn" (lovely): "det er skønt!" = it's wonderful!

examples.da: Vi rejser til Spanien næste måned.
examples.en: We are travelling to Spain next month.

examples.da: Han har boet her i tre måneder.
examples.en: He has lived here for three months.

måned.gotcha: The d is soft, almost silent: roughly "MAW-neth".

examples.da: Generalen ledede hæren under krigen.
examples.en: The general led the army during the war.

general.gotcha: Stress on the last syllable: gene-RAL, unlike English.

examples.da: Alex går i skole med min søn.
examples.en: Alex goes to school with my son.

examples.da: Der er mange muligheder for at lære dansk.
examples.en: There are many opportunities to learn Danish.

examples.da: Har du mulighed for at komme i morgen?
examples.en: Are you able to come tomorrow?

examples.da: Fader vor, du som er i himlene.
examples.en: Our Father, who art in heaven.

fader.gotcha: Formal or religious; the everyday word for father is "far".

examples.da: Vær ærlig og sig, hvad du mener.
examples.en: Be honest and say what you think.

examples.da: Helt ærligt, det var ikke min skyld.
examples.en: Honestly, it wasn't my fault.

examples.da: Vi køber friskt brød hver morgen.
examples.en: We buy fresh bread every morning.

examples.da: Kan du skære brødet ud?
examples.en: Can you slice the bread?

brød.gotcha: Also the past tense of "bryde" (to break).

examples.da: Han er meget høj for sin alder.
examples.en: He is very tall for his age.

examples.da: Musikken er for høj.
examples.en: The music is too loud.

høj.gotcha: Also means "loud" about sound; as a noun "en høj" is a hill.

examples.da: Huset ligger hundrede meter fra stranden.
examples.en: The house is a hundred meters from the beach.

examples.da: Han er næsten to meter høj.
examples.en: He is almost two meters tall.

meter.gotcha: Same form in singular and plural: en meter, to meter.

examples.da: De to søstre er helt ens.
examples.en: The two sisters are completely identical.

examples.da: Vi har ens trøjer på.
examples.en: We are wearing identical sweaters.

ens.gotcha: Also means "one's": "ens egen familie" = one's own family.

examples.da: Jeg har kun et enkelt spørgsmål.
examples.en: I have just a single question.

examples.da: Hver enkelt elev fik en bog.
examples.en: Each individual pupil got a book.

enkelt.gotcha: "Enkelt" means single/individual; "enkel" means simple — easily confused.

examples.da: Hunden løber løs i haven.
examples.en: The dog is running loose in the garden.

examples.da: Skruen er løs.
examples.en: The screw is loose.

examples.da: Cyklen er i god stand.
examples.en: The bike is in good condition.

examples.da: Han var ikke i stand til at svare.
examples.en: He was not able to answer.

stand.gotcha: Common in "i stand til" = able to, and "i god stand" = in good condition.

examples.da: Mødet begynder klokken ni.
examples.en: The meeting starts at nine o'clock.

examples.da: Hun har ni bøger med hjem.
examples.en: She has nine books with her home.

examples.da: Danny bor lige rundt om hjørnet.
examples.en: Danny lives just around the corner.

examples.da: Han er bryder og træner hver dag.
examples.en: He is a wrestler and trains every day.

bryder.gotcha: Also present of "bryde"; "jeg bryder mig ikke om det" = I don't like it.

examples.da: Kan du hjælpe mig med at trække sofaen?
examples.en: Can you help me pull the sofa?

examples.da: Lad teen trække i fem minutter.
examples.en: Let the tea steep for five minutes.

trække.gotcha: Irregular: trække, trak, trukket. "Trække vejret" means to breathe.

examples.da: Drengene slås i skolegården.
examples.en: The boys are fighting in the schoolyard.

examples.da: Hold op med at slås!
examples.en: Stop fighting!

slås.gotcha: An s-verb: "slås" is the basic form. Past tense is "sloges".

examples.da: Dengang var alting billigere.
examples.en: Back then everything was cheaper.

examples.da: Dengang jeg var barn, boede vi på landet.
examples.en: When I was a child, we lived in the countryside.
